{{Quote|And if I ever need your services again, I will not hesitate to clone you.|Palpatine|<ref name="His Finest Work" />}}
'''Garrbo V'Droz''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] renowned as [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] best [[Architect/Legends|architect]]. However, he was also very arrogant and disliked by many people because of his boastfulness, although due to his skill he was still frequently hired. At some point after the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] contracted V'Droz to construct a massive [[The Labyrinth (Mount Tantiss)|labyrinth]]. V'Droz designed the plans for a maze inside of [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] on [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], one of Palpatine's treasure vaults. As V'Droz was showing off the maze to the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] after its completion, Palpatine [[Death/Legends|killed]] him to ensure that no one else would know of the labyrinth and how to navigate it.

==Biography==
Garrbo V'Droz was a [[Color/Legends|green]]-[[Hair/Legends|haired]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] with a reputation as [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] best [[Architect/Legends|architect]]. V'Droz had designs across the galaxy, including on [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]], [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Though he was very arrogant and boastful, he had no trouble finding jobs because of his skill. He had a [[Garrbo V'Droz's brother|brother]], who worked as a construction foreman.<ref name="His Finest Work" />

At some point after [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], V'Droz was contacted by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to design plans for a maze. It was to be located at the bottom of [[Mount Tantiss/Legends|Mount Tantiss]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], one of Palpatine's storehouses and treasure vaults. V'Droz created blueprints for an extremely complicated maze per his employer's orders, and also placed [[trac-reflective]] material on the walls, preventing a [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] map from showing a correct way out.<ref name="His Finest Work" />

Palpatine was extremely pleased with V'Droz's design, and after it was finished, he traveled to Wayland to view the [[The Labyrinth (Mount Tantiss)|labyrinth]]. V'Droz explained its features to him, after which Palpatine told the architect that he wanted to see the maze. Though apprehensive, V'Droz agreed, and the two took a concealed stairway down to the lowest level. There, V'Droz saw many of the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] treasures, such as a collection of old [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]. He also saw, much to his shock, the body of his brother in a tomb. V'Droz furiously questioned his employer, who replied that with V'Droz's brother—the construction foreman on the Mount Tantiss project—dead, only the two of them knew a way through the maze, and soon, only Palpatine would know of the escape. The Emperor executed V'Droz with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], promising him that if he ever needed the architect again, he would [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] him.<ref name="His Finest Work" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|As you can see, the design fits your specifications ''perfectly''.|Garrbo V'Droz|<ref name="His Finest Work" />}}
Garrbo V'Droz was a very arrogant individual. He believed that he was the best architect in the galaxy, although many others expressed this opinion as well. V'Droz often let his arrogance go to his head, and he would get carried away with boasting about his work. Though he always tried to act confident, V'Droz was frightened when Palpatine led him down to the lowest level of Mount Tantiss, a fear which proved to be justified when the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] killed him.<ref name="His Finest Work" />

==Behind the scenes==
Garrbo V'Droz appeared in the short [[in-universe]] narrative ''[[His Finest Work]]'', featured in the [[March]] [[1994]] ''[[The Last Command Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Eric S. Trautmann]].<ref name="His Finest Work" /> This story was later included in ''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''.<ref name="His Finest Work Republished">{{WEGCite|story=His Finest Work|book=The Last Command Sourcebook|reprint=1}}</ref>
